question: [File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/health.go -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: Routes()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a DoS by sending a burst of GET /health/readiness requests that each consume a goroutine for up to 20 seconds (10s per check × 2 checks), and since Go's goroutine scheduler has no built-in limit, the process allocates unbounded goroutine stacks until memory is exhausted?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/health.go -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: checkNetworkStatus()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a DoS by exploiting the sequential execution of NetworkList followed by NetworkStatus in checkNetworkStatus(), where a slow response to the first call holds the goroutine for up to 10 seconds before the second call even starts, maximizing resource hold time per request?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/health.go -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: NewHealthController()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a DoS by exploiting the fact that health check failures with SkipOnErr: false cause the readiness endpoint to return HTTP 503, and by repeatedly triggering failures (e.g., via resource exhaustion), force the orchestrator to continuously restart the service, creating a restart loop that prevents the node from ever becoming available?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/health.go -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: checkNetworkStatus()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a DoS by sending GET /health/readiness requests that cause the internal rosettaClient to accumulate idle TCP connections to localhost:{port} that are not properly closed, exhausting the ephemeral port range and preventing new connections from being established?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/health.go -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: Routes()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a DoS by sending HTTP/1.1 pipelined requests to /health/readiness, where the server processes each pipelined request sequentially but holds the connection, causing the handler to block for up to 20 seconds per pipelined request and starving other connections?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/health.go -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: checkNetworkStatus()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a panic by sending GET /health/readiness when the internal /network/list endpoint is temporarily unavailable and returns an error, but the error path in checkNetworkStatus() logs via log.Errorf and returns the error without crashing, yet a race condition in the health-go library causes a concurrent map write panic?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/health.go -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: NewHealthController()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a DoS by exploiting the postgres health check's use of rosettaConfig.Db.GetDsn() which embeds the password in plaintext in the DSN, and by flooding /health/readiness to cause repeated failed authentication attempts that trigger PostgreSQL's connection rate limiting or account lockout, denying database access to the application?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/health.go -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: checkNetworkStatus()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a DoS by sending GET /health/readiness at a rate that causes the internal NetworkList and NetworkStatus calls to queue up in the Rosetta HTTP server's accept queue, eventually causing the accept queue to overflow and drop legitimate API requests?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/health.go -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: Routes()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a DoS by sending GET /health/readiness with a very large Accept or other HTTP header, causing the health handler to allocate large buffers for each concurrent request, leading to memory exhaustion when many such requests are in flight simultaneously?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/health.go -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: checkNetworkStatus()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a DoS by exploiting the fact that checkNetworkStatus() uses a shared rosettaClient (created once in the closure) across all concurrent goroutines, and if the underlying HTTP transport has a limited connection pool, concurrent requests serialize on transport-level locks, causing goroutine pile-up?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/health.go -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: NewHealthController()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a DoS by flooding GET /health/readiness to exhaust the PostgreSQL max_connections limit at the database server level, since each health check opens a new connection via postgres.New(), causing all other services sharing the same database to lose connectivity?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/health.go -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: checkNetworkStatus()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a DoS by sending GET /health/readiness requests that cause the health-go library to run checks concurrently with no mutex protection on shared state, triggering a data race that corrupts the health status and causes the process to crash or return incorrect results?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/health.go -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: Routes()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a DoS by sending GET /health/liveness at a very high rate, where the liveness handler (livenessHealth.HandlerFunc) has no checks but still serializes JSON responses, consuming CPU and network bandwidth that starves the main Rosetta API handlers?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/health.go -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: checkNetworkStatus()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a DoS by sending GET /health/readiness when the server is under load, causing the internal NetworkList call to time out after 10 seconds, which then causes the health check to return an error, which with SkipOnErr: false marks the service unavailable, triggering a cascade of orchestrator restarts?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/health.go -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: NewHealthController()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a DoS by exploiting the absence of a maximum concurrent health check limit, sending enough simultaneous GET /health/readiness requests to exhaust the Go runtime's goroutine scheduler capacity, causing scheduling latency that degrades all other goroutines in the process?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/health.go -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: checkNetworkStatus()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a DoS by sending GET /health/readiness requests that cause the rosettaClient to open many simultaneous connections to localhost:{port}, exhausting the loopback interface's connection tracking table and causing connection failures for all local services?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/health.go -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: Routes()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a DoS by sending GET /health/readiness with a slow HTTP client that reads the response very slowly, causing the response writer to block while holding the goroutine and all associated resources (DB connection, internal HTTP connections) for an extended period?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/health.go -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: checkNetworkStatus()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a DoS by exploiting the fact that the network check in checkNetworkStatus() calls NetworkStatus with the first network identifier from NetworkList, and if the internal server is slow to respond to NetworkStatus, the goroutine is blocked for up to 10 seconds, compounding with the postgresql check timeout for a total of 20 seconds per request?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/health.go -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: NewHealthController()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a DoS by flooding GET /health/readiness to trigger repeated postgres connection attempts that fail (e.g., due to wrong credentials or host), causing the postgres driver to log errors and retry, consuming CPU and generating excessive log output that fills disk space?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/health.go -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: checkNetworkStatus()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a DoS by sending GET /health/readiness at a rate that causes the internal Rosetta server's /network/list handler to become overloaded with health-check-originated requests, degrading its ability to serve legitimate external API requests?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/health.go -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: Routes()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a DoS by sending GET /health/readiness with HTTP/2 multiplexed streams, where many streams share a single connection and each stream triggers a full health check, bypassing per-connection rate limits and exhausting server resources more efficiently than HTTP/1.1?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/health.go -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: checkNetworkStatus()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a DoS by exploiting the fact that checkNetworkStatus() does not implement any backoff or circuit-breaker logic, so when the internal server is degraded, every health check request still attempts full NetworkList and NetworkStatus calls, amplifying load on an already-stressed system?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/health.go -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: NewHealthController()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a DoS by exploiting the sequential nature of the two readiness checks (postgresql then network), where a slow postgresql check delays the network check, causing the total check duration to approach 20 seconds and maximizing goroutine hold time per request?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/health.go -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: checkNetworkStatus()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a DoS by sending GET /health/readiness requests that cause the health-go library to allocate a new health.Check response object for each request, and under high concurrency, the garbage collector is unable to keep up with allocations, causing GC pressure that degrades all goroutines?
